capabilities: Store application secrets using a dedicated secrets manager while integrating insights from a knowledge graph to optimize security and workflow efficiency.

procedure.action: Reconcile secrets management and knowledge graph artifacts by using domain-specific tools like HashiCorp Vault and Neo4j; ensure error_count < 1 and result_valid = true.

Log operation_duration_ms and result_code to an audit trail after each merge operation to maintain traceability and compliance with security policies using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Assert the merged output by asserting all required fields pass JSON Schema draft-07 validation; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field.
Filter candidate secrets for management by querying the knowledge graph using Cypher; ensure error_count < 1 and result_valid = true.
Assign results from the knowledge graph by applying domain-specific classification algorithms; ensure error_count < 1 and result_valid = true.

summary: Integrate secrets management with knowledge graph insights to enhance security and operational efficiency by merging workflows and validating outputs.
title: Secrets Management

failureConditions: Inconsistent data when merge omits NULL handling: records with NULL key fields are silently dropped, causing referential integrity violations in downstream consumers.
Security breaches when secrets are not properly classified and stored, leading to unauthorized access.
Operational inefficiency when audit logs are incomplete, causing difficulty in tracing and compliance verification.
metrics: the merged output by asserting all required fields pass JSON Schema draft-07 validation
